title: Errors triggered by comparison operators and column references
poc: CREATE TABLE t0(c0 INT1 DEFAULT(0.051298212901439655), c1 SIGNED DEFAULT(-185766140));
CREATE VIEW v0(c0) AS SELECT t0.rowid FROM t0 WHERE 0.9268147670313996 ORDER BY t0.c1 DESC, TIMESTAMP '1970-01-17 01:38:09' DESC, t0.rowid ASC LIMIT 824793399 OFFSET 22399636;
INSERT INTO t0(c0, c1) VALUES (true, false);
SELECT t0.c1, t0.rowid, t0.c0 FROM t0;

SELECT t0.c1, t0.rowid, t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE (true<(t0.c0)) UNION ALL SELECT t0.c1, t0.rowid, t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE (NOT (true<(t0.c0))) UNION ALL SELECT t0.c1, t0.rowid, t0.c0 FROM t0 WHERE (((true<(t0.c0))) IS NULL);
key_bug_features: Inconsistent results from comparison operators in SQL queries
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c0 FLOAT, c1 BOOLEAN, c2 VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO t1(c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1.5, true, 'test1'), (2.5, false, 'test2'), (NULL, NULL, 'test3');
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT, c1 FLOAT DEFAULT 0.0, c2 BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t2(c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 0.5, true), (2, 1.5, false), (3, NULL, NULL);
SELECT t1.c2, t2.c1 FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON (t1.c0 < t2.c1) WHERE (t1.c1 IS NOT NULL) OR (t2.c2 IS NULL);
